issues.description: Content references 'Typer 0.16+' as the version for rich_markup_mode and Rich integration, but these features were actually introduced in Typer 0.12.0. This is factually incorrect and misleading.
issues.webResearchNote: Per Typer release notes (typer.tiangolo.com/release-notes/), rich_markup_mode='rich' and Rich integration were introduced in version 0.12.0 (released early 2024). Version 0.16+ appears to be a confusion with FastAPI or other framework versions. Current Typer is 0.24.x.

issues.description: Challenge ID references module-16 but this is module 18 (Professional CLI Tools with Typer). This ID appears to be a copy-paste error from a different module.
Content incorrectly states rich_markup_mode='rich' is a 'Typer 0.16+ Feature'. This feature was introduced in Typer 0.12.0, not 0.16.
issues.webResearchNote: Per Typer release notes 0.12.0 section: 'Support for Rich Markup... with the new parameter typer.Typer(rich_markup_mode="rich")'. Version 0.16 was never a major release with this feature.

issues.description: Challenge ID references module-16 but this is module 18.
Content incorrectly states Rich integration is a 'Typer 0.16+ integration'. Rich markup support was introduced in Typer 0.12.0.
issues.webResearchNote: Typer 0.12.0 release notes: 'Support for Rich Markup... with the new parameter typer.Typer(rich_markup_mode="rich")'. The 0.16+ version reference is incorrect.

issues.description: Challenge ID references module-16 but this is module 18.
Content lists 'Typer 0.16+ features used' but the features (rich_markup_mode) were introduced in Typer 0.12.0.
issues.webResearchNote: Per Typer release notes: rich_markup_mode parameter was introduced in version 0.12.0. No special 0.16+ features are required.

issues.description: Content references 'Typer 0.16+' for rich_markup_mode, but this feature was introduced in 0.12.0.
issues.webResearchNote: Typer 0.12.0 release notes confirm rich_markup_mode was introduced in that version.

issues.description: Challenge ID references module-16 but this is module 18.
